text: I didn't know my own body until I tried this. Took 30 seconds. Felt like coming home.
Started with just water tracking. Now I notice when my shoulders are tight. Small things add up.
My mother never went. I go for her.
I almost talked myself out of going. Three times. Then I went.
The app never pushed me. That's why I kept coming back.
I asked my mum. She said 'three years ago.' We booked together. Easier together.
I told my sister I was going. She said she'd come with me. We ate chicken rice after.
I didn't tell anyone I was going. Felt braver that way. Like a secret I kept for myself.
I was nervous. The nurse was kind. It took 8 minutes. Then I ate laksa.
The nurse remembered my name from last time. That helped.
I brought my sister. We went for kopi after. Made it feel less clinical.
First time I did the self-exam, I cried. Not from fear — from finally paying attention.
I do it in the shower now. Takes five minutes. I know what normal feels like.
It took twelve minutes. I had been afraid of it for six years.
My mum went first. Then I went. Then my daughter asked when she could go.
Results came back clear. I took myself for a nice lunch. Celebrated small.
I found something. They found it early. That's the whole story.
It's been three years now. I book it the same week every time. Routine, not fear.
I left a whisper here a year ago. Someone messaged me to say it helped her go. That's enough.
You don't have to be ready. You just have to be willing to try.
